scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit.
2. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court at Koramangala, Bengaluru, invoking Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
3. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court as the loan was advanced and demanded within Bengaluru.
4. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try this suit as the value of the claim falls within the limits prescribed for the City Civil Court, Bengaluru.
5. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 to adjudicate civil disputes of this nature.

LIMITATION

6. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as the loan was repayable on demand and the suit is instituted within three years from the date of advance.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant as a hand loan.
8. The amount was transferred via NEFT bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure A.
9. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages, copies of which are annexed as Annexure B.
10. The loan was advanced on the understanding that it would be repaid on demand, without any written agreement being executed between the parties.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

11. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the Plaintiff's repeated oral demands.
12. The Defendant committed default in repayment on 20.06.2024, thereby becoming liable to pay interest from the said date.
13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment, as per Annexure C.
14. The Defendant has failed to respond to the legal notice or repay the outstanding amount, constituting a breach of the obligation to repay.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

15. The principal amount outstanding as on the date of filing this plaint is Rs. 15,00,000.
16. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation.
17. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as there is no agreement for compound interest between the parties.
18. The total claim comprises the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 along with accrued interest calculated at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

LEGAL BASIS

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the money lent under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 due to the Defendant's breach of the obligation to repay.
20. The suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 as it pertains to a civil dispute regarding money recovery.
21. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 from the date of default till realisation.
22. The loan was advanced for lawful consideration via bank transfer, pre-empting any defence regarding lack of consideration or validity of the transaction.
23. The suit is filed within the limitation period as the cause of action arose recently on the date of default and demand.

CAUSE OF ACTION

24. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan upon demand.
25. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was issued and remained uncomplied with.
26. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

27. The suit is valued for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}.
28. The Plaintiff has paid ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} on the claim amount.
29. The plaint is properly stamped in accordance with the Karnataka Court Fee and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.
30. The valuation covers the principal amount and the accrued interest claimed herein.

INTEREST

31. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:
    (a) Pendente lite interest at 12% per annum from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount under Section 34 CPC.

PRAYER

32. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
33. (a) Pass a decree in fav
